title: Lesson 8 — Particles & the Map
subtitle: Date words, particle + article fusion, the map of Malta, places

sections.title: Date words
sections.subtitle: Ġranet u xhur — talking about dates
sections.intro: How to write a date in Maltese: It-Tnejn 24 ta' Frar, 2024 (Monday 24th February 2024).

sections.title: Particles + the
sections.subtitle: Partiċelli mal-artiklu — particle contractions
sections.intro: When a particle meets the article 'l-', they fuse into one word. Example: bi + l → bil-, ġo + l → ġol-.

items.en: until the / as far as the

facts: These contractions still follow the sun-consonant rule from Lesson 1.
Before sun consonants (ċ, d, n, r, s, t, x, ż, z), the L assimilates: fil-xemx → fix-xemx; ġol-sodda → ġos-sodda.
Before vowels, h, or għ — the particles bi, fi shorten to b' / f': fi dar → f'dar; bi ajruplan → b'ajruplan.
The particles sa, ma', ta' may also shorten before vowels: sa Għawdex → s'Għawdex; ma' ommi → m'ommi.

exercises.title: Combine particle + article
exercises.instructions: Pick the correct contracted form for the gap.

items.en: Sara spoke to me with proper Maltese
The rain fell into the garden
I gave the gift to the cousin
Tom is coming from the airport

sections.title: Maltese map
sections.subtitle: Mappa ta' Malta — three islands & localities
facts: Malta is a small country in the Mediterranean Sea, south of Sicily.
It is made up of three inhabited islands: Malta, Gozo (Għawdex), and Comino (Kemmuna).
Malta is the biggest island where most people live. The capital city is Valletta — a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Gozo is smaller and more rural. The capital city is Victoria, also called Rabat.
Comino is very small with permanent population in single digits — famous for the Blue Lagoon.

items.note: Walled capital — UNESCO World Heritage Site, full of museums, baroque architecture, and the famous Saluting Battery.
The 'Silent City'. Was Malta's capital before Valletta. Walking inside its walls feels like time travel.
Senglea (L-Isla), Vittoriosa (Birgu), and Cospicua (Bormla) — Malta's original harbour fortifications.
Crystal-clear shallow waters between Comino and Cominotto — Malta's most photographed swim spot.
The highest point in Malta — sheer cliffs with sea views. Especially good at sunset.
Sea caves in the south where light reflects vivid blue from the water inside.
Wide sandy beach in the north — popular for swimming and watching the sunset over the water.
The longest sandy beach on the island — calm shallow water, family-friendly.
Pilgrimage church in Gozo — visitors leave offerings of thanks for healing and answered prayers.
Dramatic coastal landscape in Gozo — inland sea, sea cliffs, the Fungus Rock.
